Patch Set 2:

(7 comments)

I'd assume that this code is already being tested by review-dne-part-[12]?  Are we sure that the bulk code will also be tested under normal test configurations?  Otherwise it should have a specific test.

Patch Set 2:

(2 comments)

Yes, these code will be tested by normal cross-MDT operation. And those bulk buffer request will be used if buffer size > 4096, which will be used when creating big stripes striped dir (already included in sanity test), or update the whole header of llog(32kB). So both inline and bulk req will be covered in our test.

”Will you show result of performance comparison?“
Oh?I do not have benchmark to show cross-MDT operation performance. But in theory, if the request is small, we should just send it inline instead of by bulk, which always request extra transfer.

Patch Set 4:

"Oh?I do not have benchmark to show cross-MDT operation performance. But in theory, if the request is small, we should just send it inline instead of by bulk, which always request extra transfer."
The theory and practice are different side of a feature. Base on my past experience for shortIO patch, there was no benefit with inline send. Probably rdma setup take much time vs one transfer. It would be great if your patch increase the efficient, but what if performance degrade?

Patch Set 5: Code-Review-1

(9 comments)

The way that the reads are submitted to disk seems incorrect.  It would also seem better to separate the change that is doing the inline request handling from the change that is doing bulk llog reads.

I think they are both important for 2.8.0 to avoid protocol changes, but it seems that they are not really related to each other and it makes reviewing the change more complex than necessary, since one part of the patch is adding bulk transfers, while the other is removing them.

(5 comments)

parents.subject: LU-6865 mdd: check return value of posix_acl_from_xattr

commit.subject: LU-6741 osp: bulk transfer for osp_md_read
commit.message:
LU-6741 osp: bulk transfer for osp_md_read

Do buffer bulk read for osp_md_read(), so it would
be more efficient to retrieve update logs from remote
target.

Signed-off-by: wang di <di.wang@intel.com>
